2605.07675.title: FactoryBench: Evaluating Industrial Machine Understanding
2605.07675.paper_summary: We introduce FactoryBench, a benchmark for evaluating time-series models and LLMs on machine understanding over industrial robotic telemetry. Q&A pairs are organized along four causal levels (state, intervention, counterfactual, decision) instantiating Pearl's ladder of causation, and span five answer formats: four structured formats are scored deterministically and free-form answers are scored by an LLM-as-judge voting protocol. We propose a scalable Q&A generation framework built around structured question templates, present FactoryWave (a dense, multitask, multivariate sensor dataset collected from a UR3 cobot and a KUKA KR10 industrial arm), and construct FactoryBench as a large-scale benchmark of over 70k Q&A items grounded in roughly 15k normalized episodes from FactoryWave, AURSAD, and voraus-AD. Zero-shot evaluation of six frontier LLMs shows that no model exceeds 50% on structured levels or 18% on decision-making, revealing a wide gap between current models and operational machine understanding.
2605.07675.paper_summary_zh: 我們介紹了FactoryBench，這是一個用於評估時間序列模型和LLMs在工業機器人遙測上機器理解的基準。問答對按照四個因果層次（狀態、干預、反事實、決策）組織，實現了Pearl的因果階梯，並涵蓋五種答案格式：四種結構化格式以確定性方式進行評分，自由形式的答案則通過LLM作為評審的投票協議進行評分。我們提出了一個可擴展的問答生成框架，圍繞結構化問題模板構建，展示了FactoryWave（從UR3協作機器人和KUKA KR10工業臂收集的密集、多任務、多變量傳感器數據集），並構建了FactoryBench，作為一個基於約15k標準化情節的超過70k問答項目的大規模基準，這些情節來自FactoryWave、AURSAD和voraus-AD。對六個前沿LLM的零樣本評估顯示，沒有模型在結構化層次上超過50%或在決策制定上超過18%，揭示了當前模型與操作機器理解之間的巨大差距。
2605.07675.author: Yanis Merzouki et.al.
2605.07675.authors: Yanis Merzouki, Coral Izquierdo, Matei Ignuta-Ciuncanu, Marcos Gomez-Bracamonte, Riccardo Maggioni, Alessandro Lombardi, Camilla Mazzoleni, Federico Martelli, Balazs Gunther, Jonas Petersen, Philipp Petersen
